name: Nebraska State Bank And Trust Company
description_short: FDIC-insured community bank offering personal and business banking, lending, and financial education with local community investment focus.
description_long:
Nebraska State Bank & Trust Company is a community-focused FDIC-insured bank operating with routing number 104110919. The bank positions itself as a locally-rooted institution emphasizing the "neighbors helping neighbors" philosophy, with leadership committed to community reinvestment and support. Since 2015, the bank has contributed $530,000 to local philanthropies and its employees completed 2,035 volunteer hours in the past year, indicating deep community engagement beyond traditional banking.

The bank offers a comprehensive suite of personal banking products including checking accounts, savings and money market accounts, Health Savings Accounts, CDs, IRAs, debit cards with tap/contactless payment upgrades, mobile banking, and Zelle transfers. For borrowing, they provide credit cards, consumer loans, and residential mortgages targeting first-time and experienced homebuyers. The agriculture and business division serves farmers, entrepreneurs, and small business owners with business checking accounts, merchant services, business debit cards, agricultural loans, and business credit cards.

NSB distinguishes itself through explicit community commitment metrics and transparency—publishing volunteer hours, philanthropic contributions, and student impact numbers on their homepage. Their three in-school savings banks program impacted 394 students this year. The bank emphasizes accessibility through mobile banking and online services ("Bank Here, Go Anywhere"), positioning digital convenience alongside traditional branch service. They maintain an education center and security resources, suggesting investment in customer financial literacy.

The company operates as a traditional community bank with standard offerings rather than innovative fintech solutions. While their community focus is genuine and verifiable, prospective customers should note this is a regional bank without national scope. The website contains limited information on specific rates, fees, minimum balances, or competitive positioning against larger institutions. As a traditional bank, they may have higher minimum deposit requirements and less competitive rates than online-only banks. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Certificates of Deposit (CDs) and Individual Retirement Accounts (IRAs)
Debit cards with contactless tap payment technology
Mobile banking and online account management
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by U.S. Government full faith and credit
Documented $530,000 in local philanthropic contributions since 2015 with transparent community impact metrics
Mobile banking and Zelle integration enable account management without branch visits
Three in-school savings banks program reaching 394 students demonstrating youth financial education commitment
Comprehensive personal and agriculture/business lending options in single institution
Contactless tap debit card technology for faster, safer payments
Explicit focus on first-time and experienced homebuyers with dedicated mortgage support
cons: Website provides no specific interest rates, APRs, or fee schedules for comparison shopping
No information on minimum account balances or opening deposit requirements
Limited details on business loan terms, SBA products, or equipment financing options
Regional community bank without national branch network or ATM access guarantees
No mention of competitive rate matching or bonus incentives for new accounts

contact.address: 945 S D St, Broken Bow, NE 68822

best_for: Rural and small-town Nebraska residents seeking community-oriented banking with local decision-making
Farmers and agricultural business owners needing specialized agricultural lending and business services
Homebuyers in Nebraska looking for personalized mortgage guidance and support
Families wanting to bank with an institution demonstrating measurable community reinvestment commitment
diagnosis: Nebraska State Bank & Trust Company is best for Nebraska residents and agricultural professionals seeking relationship-based banking from a locally-invested institution rather than a national megabank or online-only fintech. The primary caveat is that rates, fees, and specific terms are not disclosed on their website, requiring direct contact to evaluate competitive positioning against larger regional and national banks.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
address: 945 S D St, Broken Bow, NE 68822
